experiment: Product Quantization (M subspaces, K=256, scalar ADC) on Cohere 1M x 1024. src/bin/pq.rs. Trained on 100k sample, 12 kmeans iters. Recall@10 vs exact GT, with/without exact rerank.
note: PQ closes 044 open branch. RESULT: PQ wins recall-per-BYTE but loses recall-per-QPS to the binary funnel. M=32 (32 B/vec, 4x fewer than the 128 B binary code) reaches 0.992 recall (C=1000) but only 140 QPS vs the funnel 0.998 @ 963 QPS - 7x slower. M=64 0.9998 @ 62 QPS. Reason: ADC does M data-dependent gathers/doc which do NOT autovectorize, while the funnel popcount is VPOPCNTDQ (8 words/instr). PQ is cache-resident (16-64MB) so NOT bandwidth-bound - it is gather-COMPUTE-bound, and gather loses to popcount. This is 011 (asym LUT lost to popcount) confirmed at scale. PQ wins only on memory footprint (8-32 B vs 128 B). To make PQ competitive on QPS needs SIMD ADC (4-bit codes + vpshufb LUT) - the parked unsafe-SIMD path. Verdict: binary funnel stays the QPS winner; PQ is the recall-per-byte / footprint option.
